title: ADT confirms data breach after ShinyHunters leak threat
description: Home security giant ADT has confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]
author: Lawrence Abrams

title: Firestarter malware survives Cisco firewall updates, security patches
description: Cybersecurity agencies in the U.S. and U.K. are warning about a custom malware called Firestarter persisting on Cisco Firepower and Secure Firewall devices running Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) or Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) software. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: Windows Update gets new controls to reduce forced restarts
description: Microsoft is rolling out Windows Update improvements that give users more control over how updates are installed while reducing disruption from frequent or poorly timed restarts. [...]
author: Lawrence Abrams

title: New BlackFile extortion group linked to surge of vishing attacks
description: A new financially motivated hacking group tracked as BlackFile has been linked to a wave of data theft and extortion attacks against retail and hospitality organizations since February 2026. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
description: Microsoft will roll out passkey support for phishing-resistant passwordless authentication to Microsoft Entra‑protected resources from Windows devices starting late April. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: New ‘Pack2TheRoot’ flaw gives hackers root Linux access
description: A new vulnerability dubbed Pack2TheRoot could be exploited in the PackageKit daemon to allow local Linux users to install or remove system packages and gain root permissions. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
description: Article 9 of DORA makes authentication and access control a legal obligation for EU financial entities. Here is what the regulation requires, and what a breach looks like when those controls are missing. [...]
author: Sponsored by Passwork

title: Over 10,000 Zimbra servers vulnerable to ongoing XSS attacks
description: Over 10,000 Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) instances exposed online are vulnerable to ongoing attacks exploiting a cross-site scripting (XSS) security flaw. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Microsoft now lets admins uninstall Copilot on enterprise devices
description: Microsoft says IT administrators can now uninstall the AI-powered Copilot digital assistant from enterprise devices using a new policy setting, which has become broadly available after the April 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan
